title: How a restored Singapore shophouse became a home for art, Danish design and family memories
Smart Reads of the Week: Dividend Growth, Wealth Building, and Long-Term Income
article:
Investing success rarely comes from chasing the latest market trend. More often, it comes from owning quality businesses, staying disciplined, and allowing time to work its magic.
This week’s Smart Reads focuses on the building blocks of long-term wealth creation. We highlight dividend stocks with strong fundamentals, blue-chip companies aggressively buying back shares, and small-cap businesses growing their payouts while maintaining debt-free balance sheets. We also explore how investors can prepare for retirement, build wealth before 30, and identify REITs capable of delivering income for decades.
Along the way, we examine whether Genting Singapore’s share price weakness presents an opportunity and share several ideas for investors thinking about the next generation of wealth creation.
Here are this week’s top articles:
The June 2026 Watchlist: 3 Dividend Stocks Showing Strong Fundamentals
These dividend-paying companies combine solid fundamentals with attractive long-term prospects.
The “First 100k” Sprint: How to Build Your Foundation Before You Turn 30
A practical guide to building financial momentum during the most important years of your investing journey.
3 Debt-Free Small-Cap Stocks with Growing Dividends
These small-cap businesses boast strong balance sheets and rising dividends, offering an attractive combination for income investors.
June 2026: Top 3 Blue-Chip Stocks with Aggressive Share Buybacks
Share buybacks can enhance shareholder returns and signal management confidence. Here are three blue chips leading the way.
Looking for a REIT That Pays for Life? Check These 4 Signs
Learn the characteristics that separate durable income-producing REITs from the rest.
3 Stocks I’d Buy for My Children’s University Fund
A look at three quality companies that could help investors build wealth over the long term.
Traditional vs. Cashflow FIRE: Which Retirement Strategy Works Best in Singapore?
Two popular paths to financial independence. Which approach is better suited for Singapore investors?
Genting Singapore Shares Near a 10-Year Low: Opportunity?
We assess whether the market has become overly pessimistic on Genting Singapore’s prospects.
If you find investing news overwhelming, let Smart Reads help you. Each week, we send you a curated digest of the most important investing stories. Perfect for busy investors in Singapore who want critical financial news delivered in a short, easy-to-read email.
